title: • Empty gutters (space between panels) force the reader to pau
content:
• Empty gutters (space between panels) force the reader to pause, reflect, or assume meaning themselves.

2. Closure: The Reader’s Role in Comics

McCloud introduces one of the most important principles of comics: Closure.

“Closure is the phenomenon of observing the parts but perceiving the whole.”

In comics, the reader actively fills in the gaps between panels. This is a fundamental difference between comics and film:
• In film, everything is shown—there is no ambiguity between cuts.
• In comics, the reader mentally connects the missing moments between panels.

🔥 Example from Understanding Comics
McCloud shows a single panel of a man raising an axe, then the next panel of a cityscape with a scream written across it. We never see the axe hit—but we KNOW it did. This is closure in action .

🔥 Example from The Killing Joke (Alan Moore, Brian Bolland)
• The final scene (Batman and Joker laughing, then silence) leaves the reader to decide whether Batman killed Joker or not.
context: From document: 1.1.2. | Between the Frames | How Storytelling Transcends Mediums — chunk 3 (chunk 3/9)
